XYZ.title: Block Shares Climb After Q1 Report — Here's Why

macro_events.event_summary: U.S. and Iranian forces exchanged fire in the Strait of Hormuz, raising fears of a broader military conflict that could disrupt ~20% of global seaborne oil flows.
primary_drivers: Oil supply risk premium from potential Strait of Hormuz disruption
Flight-to-safety bid for gold, USD, Treasuries; risk-off in cyclicals and consumer discretionary exposed to fuel costs

beneficiaries.rationale: Largest US integrated oil major with significant upstream leverage; spot Brent spike on Hormuz disruption flows directly to upstream realizations. Limited Iranian/Gulf production exposure means it benefits from price without bearing asset risk.
Same upstream price leverage as XOM; Permian-heavy production base is geographically insulated from Middle East asset risk while realizations rise with Brent.
Pure-play US onshore (Permian) producer with high oil beta and no Middle East operating exposure; historically one of the highest-beta names to crude spikes.

Prime contractor for THAAD, PAC-3 Patriot interceptors, and JASSM — exactly the munitions consumed/restocked in a Gulf shooting conflict. Direct DoD replenishment orders historically follow such events.
Makes Standard Missile family (SM-2/3/6) used by US Navy destroyers in the Gulf, plus Tomahawks via Raytheon. Naval engagement directly consumes their inventory and triggers reorders.
TechnipFMC — subsea equipment provider; shift toward non-Gulf production (offshore Brazil, Guyana, GoM) accelerates with Hormuz risk premium, and near-term oil price strength supports project FIDs.
Scorpio Tankers — product tanker rates spike when Hormuz routing is disrupted as cargoes are rerouted longer distances (ton-mile demand). Pure-play product tanker fleet with no Gulf-flagged exposure.

losers.rationale: Delta has historically been the least-hedged US legacy carrier on jet fuel; a Brent spike of $10+ flows almost immediately into unit costs and pressures Q2 guidance. Consumer discretionary travel demand also wobbles on geopolitical fear.
Same jet fuel cost exposure as DAL plus heavier international/long-haul mix that depends on Middle East airspace routing; potential rerouting adds block hours and burn.
Carnival — bunker fuel is a top-3 cost; cruise demand also softens on geopolitical headlines. Highly levered balance sheet amplifies any margin hit.
Express air network has direct jet fuel exposure with imperfect surcharge pass-through lag; ground fleet diesel costs also rise. Industrial freight demand softens in risk-off.

Low-income consumer is most exposed to gasoline price shock; gas-pump inflation directly compresses discretionary spend on Walmart's general merchandise mix. Also bears diesel/transport cost increases.
Live Nation — discretionary consumer spend on concerts/events is sensitive to gasoline price shocks and geopolitical risk-off sentiment; international tour logistics also affected.

headlines: U.S. and Iran trade fire in Strait of Hormuz; each claims other shot first

macro_events.event_summary: Treasury yields and borrowing costs remain elevated due to a new market concern, keeping rate-sensitive assets under pressure.
primary_drivers: Higher long-end Treasury yields raise discount rates on long-duration equities
Elevated borrowing costs compress margins for leveraged/capital-intensive businesses
Higher net interest margin tailwind for banks with asset-sensitive balance sheets

beneficiaries.rationale: Schwab's earnings are highly sensitive to short-end rates via client cash sweep yields and securities reinvestment; sustained higher yields directly lift net interest revenue.
Life insurer with large fixed-income portfolio — higher reinvestment yields lift book yield and spread income on annuity/liability book.
Allstate's float is invested largely in fixed income; higher yields improve investment income, a direct earnings tailwind independent of underwriting.

losers.rationale: Zillow's transaction volume and Premier Agent revenue are tightly correlated with mortgage rates; sustained high yields freeze housing turnover.
Realty Income is a long-duration bond proxy (net-lease REIT with 10+ year leases); rising long yields directly compress equity value via higher cap rates and refinancing costs.
Crown Castle carries heavy debt load with refinancing exposure; tower REIT cash flows are long-duration and highly rate-sensitive.
Carvana relies on securitization markets and consumer auto financing; higher yields raise both its funding costs and customer monthly payments, double hit to volumes and margins.

headlines: There’s a new worry keeping Treasury yields and borrowing costs higher
